payload:
{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/7265","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/7265/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/7265/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/7265/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/7265","id":134119677,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWUxMzQxMTk2Nzc=","number":7265,"title":"Enhancement: groupby function","user":{"login":"ahed87","id":9150231,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjkxNTAyMzE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9150231?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87","html_url":"https://github.com/ahed87","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ahed87/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":31,"created_at":"2016-02-16T22:54:58Z","updated_at":"2024-10-25T17:04:00Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi,

I think it would be great with some basic groupby functionality in numpy, on int's and float's.
Using it would be something like a one liner below (just thinking of how a call could look like, not considering if it would actually work with current input/output of for example the mean function).

result = np.groupby(x, y).mean() # x.shape = (n, ) y.shape = (n, m)
or maybe,
result = np.groupby(x, y, how='mean') # reasonable options would be mean, min, max, var or std
or even,
result = np.mean(np.groupby(x, y))

result would be result.shape = (np.unique(x), m)

Background:
I recently did some cleaning of measurements signals (~5e6 samples) - all of them floats (also the groupby series).

One of the things to do with the signals was to reduce the length a bit with for example averaging, however the signal was not evenly spaced and with larger gaps in time so a simple rolling average and dropping every second sample would not work, of course the signal had some nan's scattered around as well, which does not matter until one has to use nanmean instead of mean (maybe mean with a keword like bubblenan=True could be an idea for api-simplification instead of nanmean?).

I started out with pandas since it has some great functionality for grouping, however that turned out to be like hitting a brickwall in speed (don't really understand why - but after minutes of waiting it was quite obvious..., and yes I did try some different ways) - so in the end not usable for this case.

Next step was to turn to numpy, and after a bit of searching on the web I did not really find anything that I did not already know, python loops are slow, python has itertools etc, there was some examples of grouping with numpy but they seemed to be based on integers, and it was only grouping, not including aggregating with mean in most of the cases. Quite some time ago I did some stuff with ufuncs and found them pretty slow compared to vectorized operations (I probably did something wrong), so that I never tried for this case.

In the end I wrote a small piece of code (<20 lines) based on np.diff, np.where, np.mean and np.delete in a while statement, breaking out when all elements where unique. The whole aggregation was done only on consecutive pairs, which was ensured by an internal loop over the mask dropping the multiple duplicates, leaving only consecutive pairs.

The numpy version I did was doing it in around 5 seconds per series which was good enough for me. I do not know the actual speed of pandas since I never had patience to wait for it to finish.

Maybe if the thing is really implemented in numpy it would be less than half the time,
and would be a simple oneliner.
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/7265/reactions","total_count":7,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":6,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/7265/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2438351428","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/7265#issuecomment-2438351428","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/7265","id":2438351428,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86RVkpE","user":{"login":"MLopez-Ibanez","id":2620021,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MjAwMjE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2620021?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez","html_url":"https://github.com/MLopez-Ibanez","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-25T17:03:58Z","updated_at":"2024-10-25T17:03:58Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> ```python
> def uniq_nosort(ar):
>     _, idx = np.unique(ar, return_index=True)
>     u = ar[np.sort(idx)]
>     return u
> 
> def groupby(x, groups, axis = 0):
>     for g in unique_nosort(groups):
>         yield x.compress((g == groups), axis = axis)
> ```

I wonder if there is a way to achieve the above but return views into `x` instead of copies.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2438351428/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/spelling/issues/83","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/spelling","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/spelling/issues/83/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/spelling/issues/83/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/spelling/issues/83/events","html_url":"https://github.com/ropensci/spelling/issues/83","id":2421769256,"node_id":"I_kwDOBgs76c6QWUQo","number":83,"title":"Ignore DOIs in DESCRIPTION","user":{"login":"MLopez-Ibanez","id":2620021,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MjAwMjE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2620021?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez","html_url":"https://github.com/MLopez-Ibanez","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-07-22T02:29:09Z","updated_at":"2024-07-22T02:29:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":" Using `spelling::spell_check_test(vignettes = TRUE, error = FALSE, skip_on_cran = TRUE)`,  reports "words":
```
CEC     description:1
ejor    description:1
```

```
Description: V.G. da Fonseca, C.M. Fonseca, A.O. Hall (2001) <doi:10.1007/3-540-44719-9_15>, 
C.M. Fonseca, L. Paquete, M. López-Ibáñez (2006) <doi:10.1109/CEC.2006.1688440>),
M. Binois, D. Ginsbourger, O. Roustant (2015) <doi:10.1016/j.ejor.2014.07.032>.
```

Anything between `<doi:` and `>` should be ignored.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/242","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/rcrossref","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/242/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/242/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/242/events","html_url":"https://github.com/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/242","id":2349394697,"node_id":"I_kwDOAMZlVs6MCOsJ","number":242,"title":"friendly wrappers for simple queries","user":{"login":"MLopez-Ibanez","id":2620021,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MjAwMjE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2620021?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez","html_url":"https://github.com/MLopez-Ibanez","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-06-12T17:59:10Z","updated_at":"2024-06-12T17:59:10Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"A query like:

```r
cr_cn(dois = "10.1007/s10479-023-05251-3", format="citeproc-json")
```

returns lots of data. It would be nice to have friendly wrappers for simple queries that only return the data queried as an R list:

```r
cr_cn(dois = "10.1007/s10479-023-05251-3", format="list", fields=c("title", "author", "year"))
```

or to check that a doi actually exists:

```r
doi_exists(dois = "10.1007/s10479-023-05251-3")
```

or to check that it matches some given data:

```r
doi_match(dois = "10.1007/s10479-023-05251-3", title = "A title", year = "1997", exact=FALSE)
```

or to check that a paper is retracted or not (https://www.crossref.org/documentation/principles-practices/best-practices/versioning/#00327):

```r
is_retracted(dois = "10.1007/s10479-023-05251-3")
```

Ideally, this should not require having "bibtex" installed:

```r
cr_cn(dois = "10.1007/s10479-023-05251-3", format="bibentry")
```

returns: 
```r
Error: Please install bibtex
```

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/242/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/242/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/comments/2165648196","html_url":"https://github.com/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/242#issuecomment-2165648196","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/rcrossref/issues/242","id":2165648196,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAMZlVs6BFStE","user":{"login":"MLopez-Ibanez","id":2620021,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MjAwMjE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2620021?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez","html_url":"https://github.com/MLopez-Ibanez","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-13T13:17:28Z","updated_at":"2024-06-13T13:17:28Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> 
> You can use cr_works to get parsed metadata by DOI and select the fields.

`cr_works` does not seem to return the same output as `cr_cn`

```r
cr_cn(dois="10.4230/DagRep.5.1.96")
#> [1] "@article{https://doi.org/10.4230/dagrep.5.1.96,\n  doi = {10.4230/DAGREP.5.1.96},\n  url = {https://drops.dagstuhl.de/entities/document/10.4230/DagRep.5.1.96},\n  author = {Greco, Salvatore and Klamroth, Kathrin and Knowles, Joshua D. and Rudolph, Günter},\n  keywords = {multiple criteria decision making, evolutionary multiobjective optimization},\n  language = {en},\n  title = {Understanding Complexity in Multiobjective Optimization (Dagstuhl Seminar 15031)},\n  publisher = {Schloss Dagstuhl – Leibniz-Zentrum für Informatik},\n  year = {2015},\n  copyright = {Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Germany license}\n}\n"
cr_works(filter = list(doi="10.4230/DagRep.5.1.96"))
#> $meta
#>   total_results search_terms start_index items_per_page
#> 1             0           NA           0             20
#> $data
#> # A tibble: 0 × 0
#> $facets
#> NULL
```

The use case I have is a single `foo.Rd` file has 100s of failing words (gene sequence definitions) that are valid in that context, but I do not want to bloat the WORDLIST with these idiosyncratic, highly specific set of words that are unique to `foo.Rd`. 

Function use cases:
```
spelling::spell_check_package()
spelling::spell_check_files()
```

Best case scenario is this is already possible and I've simply missed it. Thx!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/spelling/issues/63/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/spelling/issues/63/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/spelling/issues/comments/1827683461","html_url":"https://github.com/ropensci/spelling/issues/63#issuecomment-1827683461","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ropensci/spelling/issues/63","id":1827683461,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBgs76c5s8DyF","user":{"login":"MLopez-Ibanez","id":2620021,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MjAwMjE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2620021?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez","html_url":"https://github.com/MLopez-Ibanez","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-11-27T11:51:27Z","updated_at":"2023-11-27T11:51:27Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"By default, it should ignore everything ignored by `.Rbuildignore` in particular, backup files '*~'.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/19362","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/19362/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/19362/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/19362/events","html_url":"https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/19362","id":1932609607,"node_id":"I_kwDOABZIoc5zMUhH","number":19362,"title":"Warning generated by SLSQP is useless","user":{"login":"MLopez-Ibanez","id":2620021,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI2MjAwMjE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2620021?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez","html_url":"https://github.com/MLopez-Ibanez","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MLopez-Ibanez/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-10-09T09:04:39Z","updated_at":"2023-10-09T09:04:39Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/166e1f2b1ea0a1a2c3d7b030bd829549f8a5844a/scipy/optimize/_optimize.py#L422-L429

The above code generates a warning for some calls to SLSQP (see https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/11403#issuecomment-717676023). 

Users cannot do anything with this warning, as the reason for the warning is due to the implementation of SLSQP. The warning is also not informative as the result is correct always, so the message is not warning about any potential problem.

Perhaps scipy developers want to keep the warning during development in case it may catch some unexpected effects of a patch, but it should not be given to users.

[bug-19362.npy.zip](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/files/12845202/bug-19362.npy.zip)

Hopefully it works on your end!

```python
import numpy as np
from scipy.optimize import minimize
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("error")

cache = np.load("bug-19362.npy")

def fun(x):
    index = np.equal(cache[:,0:2], x).all(axis=1)
    print(index)
    f = cache[index,2]
    if (len(f) != 1):
        print(f'Could not find {x} in the cache')
        assert False
    return f[0]

res = minimize(fun, x0 = [0,30], bounds = ((0,730), (0.01,730)), method = 'SLSQP',
                       options = dict(maxiter=1000))
```

Describe your issue.

This code does not trigger any error:

```python
from scipy.stats import randint
rv = randint(1,0)
rv.stats()
```
However attempting to sample values:
```python
rv.rvs() 
```
will produce `ValueError: Domain error in arguments.`

Since the bug is in a line that is not part of the trace, it may be hard to figure out where the problem is.

It would be better to report the error when `randint()` is called.

### Reproducing Code Example

```python
from scipy.stats import randint
rv = randint(1,0)
rv.stats()
rv.rvs()
```
